channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: James doubtful for Mexico tie but Quansah fit again
item.description: Reece James misses training on the eve of England's World Cup last-16 tie against Mexico but Jarell Quansah is available again.

item.title: Unbeaten in 34 matches - why Morocco are World Cup contenders
item.description: As Morocco beat Canada to book their place in the last-8, their World Cup campaign can no longer be seen as a fairytale.

item.title: Morocco reach quarter-finals as co-hosts Canada knocked out
item.description: Canada become the first of co-hosts to exit the 2026 World Cup, after being beaten 3-0 by Morocco in Houston.

item.title: Why England won't fear Mexico or the Azteca - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why Thomas Tuchel's team will not be worried about the difficult conditions when they play Mexico at the Azteca Stadium.

item.title: Lightning, hotel security and spying - issues England face in Mexico
item.description: England are booed as they arrive at their Mexico City hotel to prepare for their World Cup last-16 game against Mexico - and it's not the only issue they face.

item.title: England have 'big concerns' - so who would you pick to face Mexico?
item.description: Who should play at right-back? Does Anthony Gordon deserve to start? Select and share your England team to face Mexico.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: Brazil going out? Chris Sutton predicts World Cup's last-16 matches
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the last-16 ties at the 2026 World Cup, including England against Mexico.

item.title: Everton agree £24m deal for Chelsea winger George
item.description: Everton agree a deal worth up to £24m including add-ons to sign Chelsea winger Tyrique George, subject to personal terms and a medical.

item.title: Premier League quartet want Scott - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Bournemouth's Alex Scott wanted by Premier League quartet, Arsenal agree to sell Leandro Trossard, Manchester United's Kobbie Mainoo on Real Madrid's radar, plus more.

item.title: Five and a half hours that left England's World Cup tie in chaos
item.description: For several hours on Friday, no-one seemed to know when England's World Cup last-16 match against co-hosts Mexico would kick off. BBC Sport explains how a story of confusion and anger - culminating in a Fifa U-turn - unfolded.

item.title: Ounahi gives Morocco the lead against Canada
item.description: Azzedine Ounahi scores the opener for Morocco against Canada in the last 32 of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Argentina overcome Cape Verde in extra-time thriller
item.description: Argentina overcome a huge scare as they beat Cape Verde 3-2 in extra time thanks to a deflected header from Cristian Romero to advance to the last 16.

item.title: 'Sensational' Messi finish gives Argentina the lead
item.description: Lionel Messi hits Argentina's opening goal against Cape Verde with a "sensational" finish in Miami, as he scores in an eighth consecutive World Cup game.

item.title: 'What a strike!' - Cabral hits brilliant goal to draw Cape Verde level in extra time
item.description: Sidny Cabral scores an incredible goal in extra time for Cape Verde as they equalise for a second time against Argentina.

item.title: Arias strike sends Colombia into last 16 with victory over Ghana
item.description: Jhon Arias scores the only goal of the game as Colombia become the last team to book their place in the last 16 following a 1-0 win over Ghana in Kansas City.

item.title: Farewell Cape Verde - the underdogs the World Cup will never forget
item.description: Cape Verde may have been the World Cup's second-smallest nation but they leave the largest impact as they depart following defeat by defending champions Argentina in an epic.

item.title: Fenerbahce complete deal to sign Ake from Man City
item.description: Fenerbahce sign Netherlands defender Nathan Ake from Manchester City in a deal that could be worth up to £8.5m.

item.title: BBC offers 'Stay Up or Catch Up' for England v Mexico
item.description: The BBC will be launching a special 'Stay Up or Catch Up' offer for its live coverage of England's game against Mexico at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Murals, flags and beef dishes - Messi mania takes hold in Miami
item.description: Lionel Messi's World Cup goals roadshow continued with his seventh of the tournament for Argentina against Cape Verde - and delighted the fans who were in Miami to see their hero.

item.title: Why Europe's top clubs are eyeing Morocco's teenage sensation Bouaddi
item.description: After lighting up the 2022 World Cup Morocco are once again shining on the global stage - but this time they have teenage sensation Ayyoub Bouaddi in their ranks.

item.title: Eight of the 10 most populous countries are not in the World Cup
item.description: Many countries with massive populations consistently miss out on football’s biggest party. What can they do to change their sporting fortunes?

item.title: World Cup quiz: How well do you remember the last 32?
item.description: The World Cup's last-32 ties were decided in a busy six-day spell. But how closely were you paying attention?

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 27
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Oxlade-Chamberlain signs new one-year Celtic deal
item.description: England-capped midfielder Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain signs a new one-year contract with Celtic.

item.title: England-Mexico 1am kick-off unchanged after Fifa U-turn
item.description: England's World Cup last-16 tie against Mexico will kick off as scheduled at 01:00 BST on Monday.

item.title: Argentina stunned as Duarte equalises for Cape Verde
item.description: Deroy Duarte stuns Argentina as he equalises for Cape Verde during their World Cup round of 32 match in Miami.

item.title: Egypt into last 16 after shootout victory against Australia
item.description: Egypt defeated Australia on penalties to claim their first ever win in the World Cup knockout stage and set up a last-16 tie with either Argentina or Cape Verde.

item.title: 'I decided last minute!' - Salah on Panenka penalty
item.description: Mo Salah explains it was a last-minute decision to "Panenka" his penalty for Egypt after they beat Australia in a shootout to progress to the last 16 of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Martinez restores Argentina's lead against Cape Verde
item.description: Lisandro Martinez restores Argentina's lead against Cape Verde in the knockout stage of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Watch the full shootout as Egypt hold nerve to make World Cup last 16
item.description: Egypt defeat Australia on penalties to claim their first ever win in the World Cup knockout stage, and make it through to the last 16.

item.title: 'History for the Pharaohs' - Egypt celebrate after winning shootout
item.description: Egypt celebrate as they win a World Cup knockout match for the first time after beating Australia 4-2 on penalties in Dallas to reach the last 16.

item.title: Ashour heads Egypt into lead against Australia
item.description: Emam Ashour heads Egypt into the lead against Australia in their 2026 World Cup round of 32 match in Dallas.

item.title: 'You can't breathe' - what is it like playing at altitude?
item.description: BBC pundits Rachel Corsie and Lucas Leiva explain their experiences of playing football at altitude, ahead of England's game at Estadio Azteca against Mexico which sits 2,240 metres above sea level.

item.title: Slot, Nuno and Kelleher pay heartfelt tributes to 'wonderful' Jota
item.description: On the first anniversary of Diogo Jota's death, Caoimhin Kelleher, Arne Slot, Nuno Espirito Santo, Conor Coady and Paulo Goncalves share their memories of the former Liverpool and Wolves forward.

item.title: Williamson eyes Scotland job with McCoist on staff
item.description: Former Hibernian and Kilmarnock manager Bobby Williamson puts himself forward for the job as Scotland head coach leading a dream team of Ally McCoist, Scott Brown and Kevin Thomson.

item.title: England fan tickets for Mexico on resale for up to £26K
item.description: Tickets originally bought by England fans for the World Cup last-16 game against Mexico are relisted for thousands of pounds on Fifa's official resale portal.

item.title: After World Cup goals are last year's signings finally set to deliver for Newcastle?
item.description: Anthony Elanga and Yoane Wissa both struggled in their first season at Newcastle, but five goals between at the World Cup them suggest things will be different next term.

item.title: Rice likely to be available for England v Mexico
item.description: Declan Rice is likely to be available to start England's World Cup tie against Mexico despite an ongoing injury issue.

item.title: Ex-Spurs boss Postecoglou takes over at Ronaldo's Al-Nassr
item.description: Former Tottenham boss Ange Postecoglou is appointed as head coach of Al-Nassr in the Saudi Pro League - where he will manage Cristiano Ronaldo.

item.title: Germany want Klopp talks as Nagelsmann resigns
item.description: The German football association is seeking talks with Jurgen Klopp about becoming their men's national team coach after Julian Nagelsmann resigned.

item.title: Arsenal sign Lioness Stanway from Bayern
item.description: England midfielder Georgia Stanway says she is joining Arsenal to "win trophies and grow as a player".

item.title: Mexico's unlikely hero who England could fear most
item.description: Julian Quinones' path to being a Mexican hero has been unorthodox - and he will hope to become a legend against England on Monday morning.

item.title: Xhaka set to stay at Sunderland despite Chelsea interest
item.description: Sunderland captain Granit Xhaka is set to stay at the club this summer, despite interest from Chelsea.

item.title: How to turn BBC Sport alerts on or off for England v Mexico
item.description: Find out how to turn BBC Sport alerts off (or on!) for England's World Cup last-16 match against Mexico.

item.title: Chelsea sign Japan midfielder Matsukubo
item.description: Chelsea sign Japan international Manaka Matsukubo on a five-year contract from North Carolina Courage.

item.title: Bournemouth agree £25.7m deal for Elche striker Rodriguez
item.description: Bournemouth enter talks for Elche striker Alvaro Rodriguez for an initial fee of £21.4m, rising to £25.7m with add-ons.

item.title: Secret location & sleep devices: England bid to combat noisy Mexico fans
item.description: England are taking precautions to ensure boisterous locals do not disrupt their preparations in Mexico.

item.title: What you need to know about England's last-16 opponents Mexico
item.description: England will face Mexico in the World Cup round of 16. Here is what you need to know about them.

item.title: First Afcon, now World Cup - Senegal trapped in 'football hell'
item.description: Senegal's nightmarish year continues, with a heart-breaking collapse against Belgium featuring late penalty drama which was remarkably similar to events in this year's chaotic Afcon final.

item.title: Why Spurs' statement spending appears set to continue
item.description: Tottenham are set to break their record transfer for the second time in a matter of days with the club's impressive performance off the field allowing greater flexibility for investment into the first-team squad.

item.title: USA march on but will Balogun red card prove costly?
item.description: Mauricio Pochettino's USA side set up a World Cup last-16 meeting with Belgium, but how costly will forward Folarin Balogun's red card prove?

item.title: England into last 16 - what is their path to the final?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after progressing the last 16

item.title: Pampered, protected & primed - so why didn't Scotland players perform?
item.description: Scotland's players got everything they asked for at this World Cup. So why did they not perform on the pitch in the US?

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: 'Celtic in fresh talks for Qarabag's Duran' - gossip
item.description: Celtic are reportedly close to signing Qarabag forward Camilo Duran amid transfer speculation for Aberdeen, Dundee, Hearts, Rangers and St Mirren.

item.title: Denmark defender Thomsen signs two-year London City deal
item.description: London City Lionesses sign Denmark international defender Janni Thomsen from Utah Royals FC.

item.title: Aston Villa sign Norway midfielder Kielland
item.description: Norway midfielder Justine Kielland joins Aston Villa from German club Wolfsburg, subject to visa approval. Kielland joined Norwegian side Brann in 2023. During her time there, she helped the club reach the quarter-finals of the Women's Champions League, playing in 12 matches and scoring four goals.

item.title: WSL newcomers Crystal Palace agree deal for Enge
item.description: Women's Super League newcomers Crystal Palace are set to sign midfielder Sierra Enge from French side Strasbourg.

item.title: Bronze renews contract with Chelsea until 2027
item.description: England international Lucy Bronze has signed a contract extension with Chelsea until 2027.

item.title: Maddy Cusack's coach her 'nemesis', mum tells inquest
item.description: Cusack would still be alive if the coach of the women's team had not been employed, her mother says.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
